3.happening: Create an empty set to remember values we've already seen.
3.why: A hash set gives O(1) membership checks — far cheaper than rescanning.
3.invariant: seen holds every value encountered so far.

4.happening: Take the next value from the array.
4.why: We test each value exactly once, in order.
4.invariant: num is the value under consideration.

5.happening: Have we seen {num} before? Look it up in the set.
5.why: If it's already there, we've found a repeat.
5.invariant: A hit means a duplicate exists.

6.happening: {num} is already in the set — a duplicate exists, return true.
6.why: The same value appeared earlier, so the array is not all-distinct.
6.invariant: The answer is true the instant any value repeats.

7.happening: New value — add {num} to the set and keep scanning.
7.why: Recording it now lets a later repeat be caught in O(1).
7.invariant: Every value seen so far is now in the set.

8.happening: Scanned every value with no repeat — return false.
8.why: No value ever matched an existing entry, so all elements are distinct.
8.invariant: The set holds n distinct values.

init.happening: Preparing an empty set of seen values.
init.why: We'll record each value as we encounter it.

loop.happening: Walking the array value by value.
loop.why: Each value is checked exactly once.
loop.invariant: Values before the cursor are all in the set.

check.happening: Looking up the current value in the set.
check.why: An existing entry means a duplicate.

update.why: So a future repeat is caught instantly.

done.happening: A duplicate was found — returning true.
done.why: Two equal values exist in the array.
